parameters.formDescription:
=About the job
Knowledge Services is seeking a Quality Assurance Engineer (QA) who thrives in the space between fully automated testing and hands-on manual testing.

This role is ideal for someone who values software quality, is curious by nature, and is willing to jump in wherever needed - whether that’s validating acceptance criteria, executing manual test cases, assisting with automation efforts, or supporting load/performance testing.
This is not a pure automation role. We’re looking for someone who prefers manual testing but is comfortable with scripting, understands automated test frameworks, and can collaborate closely with developers who own unit testing.


Quality Assurance Engineer Responsibilities:

Perform manual testing across web, API, and backend systems in an Angular/.NET stack, validating acceptance criteria, and uncovering edge cases.
Contribute to automated test creation when needed, especially around integration, API, and regression workflows.
Support load and performance testing, working with shared IT services or internal tooling.
Review user stories and acceptance criteria to ensure clarity, testability, and alignment with business requirements.
Assess blast radius and potential risk when code changes are introduced.
Collaborate with developers to ensure effective unit test coverage.
Maintain well-organized test cases, documentation, and test artifacts.
Participate fully in agile ceremonies (standups, backlog refinement, sprint planning, retrospectives).
Work cross-functionally to champion quality and continuously improve our testing practices.
Seek out opportunities to improve test coverage, processes, and team efficiency.


Quality Assurance Engineer Required Skills & Experience:

Strong background in manual software testing.
Ability to write or understand scripts in languages such as Python, JavaScript, Bash, or PowerShell.
Experience with automated testing tools or frameworks (such as Cypress, Playwright, Selenium, or similar).
Familiarity with Git and standard branching workflows.
Ability to read and interpret acceptance criteria, user stories, and technical documentation.
Experience working in an agile environment (Scrum or Kanban).
Strong organizational skills with the ability to track multiple test efforts simultaneously.
Analytical mindset with a natural inclination to “seek out quality” - someone who is curious, thorough, and thoughtful.
Willingness to perform manual testing as the primary focus of the role.
Eagerness to learn new tools, platforms, and testing methods.


Preferred Qualifications

Candidates who have transitioned from development to QA, or who have technical backgrounds but prefer quality-focused work.
Experience developing or maintaining automated test suites.
Exposure to load/performance testing tools (JMeter, k6, Locust, etc.).
Understanding of CI/CD pipelines and how automated tests integrate within them.

nodes.name: Append or update row in sheet

options.systemMessage:
=Compare the following job description and resume.

Evaluate the match using the following weighting:
- Skills match: 40%
- Experience match: 30%
- Tools/technologies match: 20%
- Domain relevance: 10%

Assign a qualification score between 0 and 1 where:
1 = Excellent match
0 = No match

Return ONLY in this format:

Score: <number>
Reason: <max 20 words>

parameters.subject: Rejection letter
parameters.message:
=Hi {{ $('On form submission').item.json.Name }},  
Thank you for your interest in the QA Engineer role at ABC Solutions. After reviewing your application, we have decided to move forward with other candidates at this time.  We appreciate your time and wish you the best in your job search.  
Best regards, 
ABC Solutions

nodes.name: Append or update row in sheet2

values.content:
=Given the following job description and candidate CV, generate 5 professional screening questions to assess the candidate's fit.

Focus on:
- technical skills
- relevant experience
- tools and technologies
- problem-solving
- domain relevance

Rules:
- return ONLY raw valid JSON
- no markdown
- no code fences
- no explanation
- each question must be under 25 words

Return exactly:
{
  "q1": "",
  "q2": "",
  "q3": "",
  "q4": "",
  "q5": ""
}

Job Description:
{{ $('Append or update row in sheet2').item.json['CV Score Notes'] }}

Candidate CV:
{{ $('Extract from File').item.json.text }}

options.systemMessage:
You are an interview evaluation assistant.

You will receive 5 interview questions and the candidate's answers.

Evaluate each answer based on:
- Technical accuracy
- Depth of explanation
- Relevance to the question
- Practical experience

Scoring rules:
0–10 = Very weak / incorrect answer
11–20 = Basic understanding
21–30 = Good understanding
31–40 = Strong technical answer
41–50 = Excellent expert-level answer

IMPORTANT:
Each question must be scored from 0 to 50.

Return ONLY valid JSON.
Do not include explanations, markdown, or text outside JSON.

Output format MUST be exactly:

{
  "scores": {
    "question1_score": <number>,
    "question2_score": <number>,
    "question3_score": <number>,
    "question4_score": <number>,
    "question5_score": <number>,
    "total_score": <number_out_of_50>
  }
}

Rules:
- Each question score must be between 0 and 50.
- total_score must represent the overall evaluation out of 50.
- total_score should reflect the overall quality of the candidate's answers.

nodes.name: Append or update row in sheet4

values.content:
=You are an AI recruiting assistant.

Your task is to generate a professional email inviting a candidate for a phone interview.

The candidate has answered several interview questions and received a score a good score.

Instructions:
- Write a professional and friendly email.
- Mention a strength from the candidate’s responses or background.
- Politely invite them for a phone conversation.
- Keep the tone warm, professional, and concise.

Provide an output of

To:
Subject:
Email Content:

Inputs:

The candidate's name:{{ $('On form submission').item.json.Name }}
The job title: QA Engineer
The total interview score:{{ $('If1').item.json.output.scores.total_score }}

Example email:

Example email:
Dear [Candidate's Name],

Thank you for submitting your application and responses to the questionnaire for the [Job Title] position. We were impressed by [specific strength or achievement from their CV or questionnaire, e.g., "your experience in automating workflows using n8n, which aligns closely with our goals"].

We’d love to continue the conversation to discuss your experience further.

Looking forward to speaking with you soon.

=Given the job description, along with the candidate's CV and their responses to the questionnaires, generate a list of screening questions that will help gauge the candidate's suitability for the role. The questions should focus on understanding the candidate’s relevant experience, skills, and cultural fit. The questions should take into account both the job description and the candidate's background and responses. Provide a minimum of 5 questions.

Use the tools to get the job description and the applicant's responses to the questionnaires.

Applicant's CV:
{{ $('Extract from File').item.json.text }}

Give the output as various sentences as a paragraph with every new question in a new line:

Screening Questions:
